Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Wyatt A. Lison of Feinstein of Doyle Payne & Kravec LLC, Tel. No.
1-415-281-8400 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Jacob M. Harper of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP, Tel. No. 1-213-633-6863 appearing
for the Defendant.


RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant NuttZo LLC's anti-SLAPP motion to strike both causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Howard Clark is denied as to both causes of action. The commercial speech exemption per CCP 425.17(c) applies to Mr. Clark's UCL and CLRA claims and neither of those claims arise from protected speech in the anti-SLAPP statute. Mr. Clark has shown that 1) NuttZo is a corporation primarily in the business of selling nut and seed butter products at issue in this case; 2) the alleged violations of CLRA and UCL arise from NuttZo's "no added sugar" label, which is a statement of fact regarding NuttZo's product; 3) NuttZo's label is made to promote and sell the product; and 4) the label is intended for potential consumers of the product. The "no added sugar" statement is not protected per CCP 425.16(e)(2) because it was not made in connection with an issue under consideration or review by the FDA or any other official body. The statement is also not protected under CCP 425.16(e)(4) because Mr. Clark's claims do not concern public matters of health and nutrition, but rather alleged mislabeling in violation of applicable law. Mr. Clark's request for attorneys' cost and fees is denied. Mr. Clark has not shown that Nuttzo's motion is frivolous. Evidentiary objections were ruled on the record. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
